sections.paragraph:
ἐπίβλημα, ατος, τό (s. βάλλω; Nicostr. Com. 15; Plut., Arrian et al.; as a piece of clothing used as a covering as early as SIG 1218, 4 [c. 420 b.c.], also Is 3:22; lit. ‘that which is thrown over’) a piece of cloth used to repair a hole in clothing, a patch Mt 9:16; Mk 2:21; Lk 5:36. B-D-F §272.—M-M.

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 368
